_description: Cross-run technique registry. Written ONLY after human verdict. Never from automated reviews.

techniques.note: 3D depth consistently rated better than flat layouts
Subtle grain improves perceived craft quality
Good for animation but doesn't improve static screenshot quality
Novel layout encoding rank in visual structure — interesting approach
Default AI slop pattern. Every centered dark card was rated bad.
All 3 V1 concepts converged on this. Looks generic and AI-generated.
All 3 designers independently chose this. Banned aesthetic.
Part of the fight card convergence. Banned aesthetic.
Strong when combined with depth. Weak alone.
Data viz encoding rank as bar length — novel, GPT-5.4 winner.

verdicts.feedback: All 3 concepts visually identical — gold/dark convergence. No diversity despite different approach docs.
V2 is worse than V1. Quality didn't improve. Pressure Print broken.
Builder 1 built Designer 0's concept due to race condition (missing approach doc). All builds used same fonts/colors.
Brief drift — moodboard of Spotify Wrapped caused builders to make music recaps instead of sneaker sharecards. Content mismatch.
All 3 designers chose vintage boxing fight card concept. Conceptual convergence not caught by gate (gate only checked fonts/colors mechanically).
Concepts functional but research phase was performative — moodboard was aggregator thumbnails, not real designs. Concept 1 treemap interesting.
Designers burned all Hermes turns on mandatory browser research. Approach docs never written. Research succeeded with Refero but designers failed.
All 3 built successfully. Concept 0 (Opus) best. But builds feel flat — approach docs are rich and creative but output doesn't translate the energy.

learnings.feedback: 0 great, 6 acceptable, 9 bad. Creative ambition > polish. AI slop = instant fail.
Moodboard visual content overwhelms brief text. Content fidelity must be enforced at both designer and builder level.
LLMs converge on the same aesthetic local optimum. Gate must check conceptual convergence, not just fonts/colors.
Designers should NOT do browser research. It burns all Hermes turns. Research node does the research, designers just design.
Approach docs are rich and creative (25KB) but builders only saw 3KB. Full creative narrative must reach the builder.
